message:
fix(abi): fall through to host pthread_sigqueue when no native resolution (bd-l2yj)

Unblock the pthread_sigqueue_preserves_siginfo_and_ucontext_after_deferred_replay
regression (bd-l2yj) by restoring the host-fallback path for
pthread_sigqueue in LD_PRELOAD / host mode. Previously the None arm
of the native-thread match returned ESRCH unconditionally, which
matched the kernel behavior only for a truly-gone thread but caused
host threads that had never been registered with the native
thread-table to incorrectly see ESRCH instead of the real glibc
delivery result.

crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:
- Add ResolvedPthreadSigqueueFn function-pointer alias and
  RESOLVED_PTHREAD_SIGQUEUE_PTR OnceLock cache matching the pattern
  used by the rest of the pthread host-fallback resolvers in this
  module.
- Add resolved_pthread_sigqueue_fn() that resolves "pthread_sigqueue"
  through resolve_cached_pthread_attr_symbol so the host libpthread
  symbol is looked up exactly once and reused.
- In the None arm of pthread_sigqueue, try the resolved host symbol
  first and only fall back to ESRCH if the host function is itself
  unresolvable. When the host function is found, return its real
  errno (which includes ESRCH when appropriate and allows the glibc
  path to deliver the signal with siginfo / ucontext intact so the
  deferred-replay test can observe them).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
